title: Peppy Canadian Sevaiiya Stir-fry
description: A peppery, flaky, energizing Canadian dish featuring sevaiiya, prepared with Mediterranean-friendly ingredients and cooked using the pressure-cooking method for quick meal prep.

ingredients.name: Tomato puree
ingredients.notes: Ensure it's fully cooked and not raw.

ingredients.name: Chickpeas (Garbanzo beans)
ingredients.notes: Choose canned or soaked overnight for easier cooking.

ingredients.name: Onion, finely chopped
ingredients.notes: Optional, use red onion for a better flavor profile.

ingredients.name: Cumin seeds
ingredients.notes: Whole or ground both work well.

instructions: In a large pot, add enough water and sevaiiya, bring to boil, then reduce heat to low and let it simmer for about 10-15 minutes until tender but not mushy.
Drain the cooked sevaiiya and keep aside. In another pan, lightly sauté garlic and onion (if using), followed by cumin seeds and fennel seeds.
Add tomato puree to the pan with a pinch of salt, mix well and cook until thickened slightly. If desired, you can add more water for thinning it down or thicken further with cornstarch.
Fold in the cooked sevaiiya into the tomato sauce, ensuring all pieces are coated evenly. Add turmeric powder to prevent browning and adjust salt if needed.
Serve hot as a side dish or as an appetizer with dips. Can be prepared ahead and reheated for convenience.
